name: overnight_with_adaptive_setback_and_wakeup
description: Warm summer day: afternoon cooling (14:00–22:00), bedtime at 22:30 applies cool setback (default depth = comfort_cool + 3F = 77F, clamped to setback_cool=79F), morning wakeup at 07:00 restores comfort. Full overnight cycle.
notes: Bedtime target = comfort_cool(74) + 3 = 77F. This is below the ceiling setback_cool(79), so 77F applies.
No thermal model configured — uses default setback depth path.
Covers Priority 5 overnight bedtime/wakeup from plan abundant-mapping-clarke.md.

events.note: Afternoon classification — cool mode starts at comfort_cool=74F
Evening: indoor cooling down, outdoor still warm — AC running normally
Bedtime: cool setback fires. Default depth = comfort_cool(74) + 3F = 77F. Ceiling=79F so 77F applies.
Overnight: indoor 76.5F with setback target 77F — AC barely running

assertions.reason: Afternoon classification: cool mode at comfort_cool=74F
Bedtime cool setback: comfort_cool(74) + DEFAULT_SETBACK_DEPTH_COOL_F(3) = 77F (below ceiling 79F)

verdict.summary: Overnight cycle: cool classification → bedtime setback (77F) → morning comfort restore (74F)
verdict.observed_behavior: classification_applied (74F) at 14:00; setback_applied (77F) at 22:30; comfort_restored (74F) at 07:00
verdict.expected_behavior: Standard overnight cycle with default-depth cool setback and morning comfort restore
